title: Why You're Still Better Than AI at Editing Documents - CS50 Tech Talk
summary: AI struggles reliably to edit complex Word documents because their internal structure operates like software with its own logic and rules, not like plain text files. Humans excel at single-document editing while maintaining coherence, whereas computers can scale to thousands of documents only if every change is verifiable and trusted. The speaker argues that treating documents as software rather than files is necessary for reliable AI document editing at scale.
